name: Toasted Tongues
recipeInstructions:
1. With an adult's help, position an oven rack on the lowest shelf and preheat the oven to 200°C (400°F).
2. Separate the egg whites into two small bowls, making sure there are no yolks in the bowl of whites.
3. Rapidly beat the egg whites until they form stiff peaks.
4. Slowly stir spoonfuls of sugar into the whites while continuing to beat until the entire cup of sugar has been added. The mixture should now form stiff, shiny peaks.
5. Add 2–3 drops of red food coloring and mix gently.
6. Place a sheet of parchment paper on a cookie sheet.
7. To form tongues: Spoon about 3 tablespoons of meringue in the shape of a tongue onto the parchment paper. Tongues should be the length of the popsicle sticks.
8. Press a popsicle stick gently into the center of each tongue, leaving about 2 inches (5 cm) of stick hanging off.
9. Bake for about 3 hours or until the tongues are completely dry to the touch. Allow them to cool completely before carefully lifting them off the paper.
10. Makes about 12 little lickers.
Sicko serving suggestion: To serve coated tongues, use a toothpick to spread a thin layer of mucus (marshmallow topping) down the length of the lickers.
